title: Messages and Roles
summary: A chat completions request sends messages. The supported helper classes include SystemMessage, UserMessage, AssistantMessage, ToolMessage, and DeveloperMessage in azure.ai.inference.
analogy: Imagine a courtroom transcript. The judge, witness, lawyer, and clerk all speak, but the role label tells you how to interpret each line. In chat inference, system is policy, user is request, assistant is model history, and tool is external result.
diagram:
flowchart TD
  System[System rules] --> User[User asks]
  User --> Assistant[Assistant replies]
  Assistant --> Tool[Tool result when requested]
  Tool --> Assistant2[Assistant final answer]
body:
## First principles

Chat models do not read your mind; they read an ordered transcript. Each item has a role so the model can separate instructions, user input, prior assistant output, and tool results.

## Definition

A chat completions request sends messages. The supported helper classes include SystemMessage, UserMessage, AssistantMessage, ToolMessage, and DeveloperMessage in azure.ai.inference.models. Dictionaries with role and content are also accepted.

## How it fits together

Build messages from highest authority to newest conversation turn. Put stable behavior in SystemMessage, the learner request in UserMessage, previous model replies in AssistantMessage, and function outputs in ToolMessage only after the model asks for a tool call.

## Worked example

```python
import os
from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential
from azure.ai.inference import ChatCompletionsClient
from azure.ai.inference.models import SystemMessage, UserMessage, AssistantMessage

client = ChatCompletionsClient(
    endpoint=os.environ["AZURE_AI_CHAT_ENDPOINT"],
    credential=DefaultAzureCredential(),
)

messages = [
    SystemMessage("You are concise and explain from first principles."),
    UserMessage("What is a managed identity in one analogy?"),
    AssistantMessage("It is like an employee badge for Azure resources."),
    UserMessage("Now make the analogy even simpler."),
]

response = client.complete(messages=messages, model=os.getenv("AZURE_AI_MODEL_DEPLOYMENT_NAME"))
print(response.choices[0].message.content)
```

*Send typed messages with managed identity.*

drills.q: Where do durable behavior instructions belong?
drills.a: In a system or developer message, depending on model support and your policy.
They preserve conversation history so the next answer has context.
After the model requests a tool call and your code has run the tool.

teachItBack: A chat request is an ordered role-labeled transcript. Roles tell the model how to treat each line. Typed helper classes reduce mistakes, but dictionaries work too.

glossary.definition: Instruction message that shapes model behavior.
Message carrying a tool result back to the model.

sources.title: Azure AI Inference client library for Python

choices: It returns iterable updates with deltas
quiz.explanation: Streaming returns partial updates as the model generates content.
